framework_bugs.summary: start_phase4 blocks production-deployment projects (poc_mode='production' is treated as a POC mode)
framework_bugs.reproduction: init.sh --non-interactive --deployment organizational --gov-mode production ... ; advance through phases 1-3; bash scripts/process-checklist.sh --start-phase4
framework_bugs.expected: Phase 4 (production release) should be allowed for projects initialized with --gov-mode production. The POC-block in start_phase4 should treat 'production' as not-a-POC.
framework_bugs.actual: init.sh writes .claude/phase-state.json '.poc_mode = "production"'. start_phase4() checks `if [ -n "$poc_mode" ] && [ "$poc_mode" != "null" ]; then print_fail ... ; exit 1`. So production projects are blocked from Phase 4 with message 'Phase 4 is blocked — project is in production mode.' suggesting the user run `upgrade-project.sh --to-production` (which is the only path that clears poc_mode). This is a Phase-4-unreachable bug for the production scenario unless the user happens to run the upgrade script. process-checklist.sh start_phase4() must either (a) skip the block when poc_mode == 'production', or (b) init.sh should write poc_mode=null for --gov-mode production instead of the literal 'production'.

documentation_gaps.gap: init.sh skips release workflow for mcp_server platform without explaining how to add one or that it's intentional. Output reads: 'No release pipeline template for platform mcp_server on host github. Skipping release pipeline.' — no link to docs or remediation hint.

Platform module mcp_server.md is missing. verify-install.sh raises a manual action item: 'Platform module (mcp_server.md) missing → Copy from orchestrator: docs/platform-modules/mcp_server.md' but the source path also has no mcp_server.md (verified via Glob). Either ship the module or downgrade the verification finding to INFO for unsupported platforms.

tests.name: phase_state_advances_to_2
tests.details: U-D confirmation: no script auto-advances .current_phase after phase 1 completion. process-checklist.sh provides --start-phase1/--start-phase3/--start-phase4 but no --advance-phase or hook in --complete-step that bumps current_phase. Manually edited phase-state.json to continue testing.

framework_bugs.summary: U-D confirmation: no script advances current_phase after phase 1/2/3 gates pass
framework_bugs.reproduction: Complete all PHASE1_STEPS via --complete-step phase1_architecture:<step>; bash scripts/check-phase-gate.sh; jq '.current_phase' .claude/phase-state.json
framework_bugs.expected: After all phase 1 steps complete and the gate passes (or after a dedicated --advance-phase command), .current_phase should bump from 0 to 1 (and similarly 1->2, 2->3, 3->4).
framework_bugs.actual: current_phase remains at its initial value. Tested manually editing phase-state.json to continue testing later phases. Per runbook, this is U-D from prior sweep and is still open.

tests.name: each_step_completes_in_order

tests.name: pre_commit_passes_after_loop
tests.details: git commit -m 'feat(uat-1): stub feature for UAT' succeeded when all 5 steps were complete.

tests.name: bl006_blocks_feat_without_loop
tests.details: U-C confirmation: direct shell `git commit --allow-empty -m 'feat(bl006-trigger): no loop'` succeeded (rc=0) with no Build Loop active. The PreToolUse hook is what catches feat: commits via the gate; shell commits bypass it. Recorded as Medium per runbook (still-open prior-sweep bug). bl006 check-commit-message via `process-checklist.sh --check-commit-message` correctly blocks (rc=1) when no Build Loop active and current_phase>=2 — gate logic itself is sound.

tests.name: bl006_grace_window_resets_after_record
tests.details: After --record-feature uat-feat-1 + --complete-step build_loop:feature_recorded, started uat-feat-2 with full Build Loop, then `process-checklist.sh --check-commit-message 'feat(uat-2): grace window restored'` passed (rc=0). PR #17 grace-window auto-reset is working.

tests.name: bl015_sentinel_blocks_commit
tests.details: Wrote .claude/pending-approval.json with valid schema. Invoked pre-commit-gate.sh via stdin JSON ({"tool_name":"Bash","tool_input":{"command":"git commit -m feat(test):msg"}}); response was {"permissionDecision": "deny", "permissionDecisionReason": "pre-commit gate: commit blocked — pending user decision."} (PR #16 BL-015 fix confirmed).

tests.name: bl015_sentinel_resolves
tests.details: scripts/pending-approval.sh --resolve removed the sentinel; subsequent gate invocation no longer returned BL-015 deny (deny shifted to a different reason — phase2_init verification — which is expected).

tests.name: recorded_feature_appears_in_progress
tests.details: jq '.features_completed' .claude/build-progress.json == ['uat-feat-1','uat-feat-2'].

framework_bugs.summary: U-C confirmation: direct shell git commit bypasses BL-006 enforcement
framework_bugs.reproduction: After project init and Phase 2 start: git commit --allow-empty -m 'feat(bl006-trigger): no loop'
framework_bugs.expected: feat(...) commit with no Build Loop active should be blocked.
framework_bugs.actual: Commit succeeds (rc=0). The pre-commit-gate.sh logic is gated on Claude Code's PreToolUse hook receiving the bash command via stdin; a direct shell commit never calls the hook. The git commit-msg hook is not installed by init.sh — there is .git/hooks/pre-commit (gitleaks/Semgrep) but no commit-msg hook bridging to scripts/process-checklist.sh --check-commit-message. Per runbook, this is U-C (prior-sweep) still open.

documentation_gaps.gap: BL-006 enforcement boundary not documented for end users — direct shell commits bypass the gate. Either install a git commit-msg hook on init.sh or document the agent-only enforcement path prominently in CLAUDE.md / builders-guide.md.
documentation_gaps.where: init.sh git hook installation; docs/reference/builders-guide.md BL-006 section

summary: BL-016 --non-interactive init worked end-to-end for production/mcp_server/standard. PR #16/#17/#18/#19 fixes confirmed (BL-015 sentinel, BL-006 grace-window auto-reset, helper scripts copied, framework-self guard, fake-remote tolerance). One Critical bug found: start_phase4 incorrectly blocks projects initialized with --gov-mode production because init.sh writes phase-state.poc_mode='production' and start_phase4 treats any non-null poc_mode as a POC. U-C and U-D prior-sweep bugs re-confirmed. Documentation gaps around mcp_server release-pipeline absence and platform-module absence.
